diagrams.title: Basic Graph Structure
diagrams.description: Shows fundamental components of a graph: vertices and edges connecting them
elements: Vertices (circles): V = {v1, v2, v3, v4, v5}
Edges (lines): E = {e1, e2, e3, e4, e5}
Edge v1-v2 means edge connects vertices v1 and v2

diagrams.title: Types of Graphs Overview
diagrams.description: Visual comparison of simple graph, multigraph, pseudograph, and digraph
elements: Simple graph: single edge between any pair, no self-loops
Multigraph: multiple edges (parallel edges) between vertices
Pseudograph: parallel edges + self-loops (edge from vertex to itself)
Digraph: arrows showing direction between vertices

diagrams.title: Complete Graph K5
diagrams.description: Complete graph with 5 vertices where every pair is connected

diagrams.title: Cycle and Path Graphs
elements: C5: 5 vertices in circle, each connected to 2 neighbors, 5 edges
P5: 5 vertices in line, 2 endpoints degree 1, 3 internal degree 2, 4 edges
Both are regular: C5 is 2-regular, P5 is not regular
diagrams.purpose: Understanding cycle and path graph structures

diagrams.title: Vertex Degree Examples
diagrams.description: Shows isolated vertex, pendant vertex, and regular vertex examples
elements: Regular vertex: all vertices have same degree in regular graph
